rationale_for_baseline_tier: Acute severe symptomatic hypocalcaemia with active tetany (carpopedal spasm, perioral numbness, facial twitching) and subjective breathing difficulty in a patient who is alone, following abrupt cessation of calcium/calcitriol after total thyroidectomy. This carries an immediate risk of laryngospasm, generalised seizure and QT-prolongation arrhythmia — an immediate threat to life. T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance) is the only appropriate tier; the definitive treatment (IV calcium with cardiac monitoring) and airway backup are hospital resources. The T5 ceiling is justified because the patient can themselves report time-critical emergency features (locked tetanic spasm, perioral numbness, sensation of not being able to breathe) — not merely because an exam or test is needed.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: High-pitched wheeze or stridor, throat feeling like it is closing, or severe difficulty breathing
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately tell the 000 dispatcher to upgrade the ambulance priority
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals laryngospasm and impending complete airway obstruction from severe hypocalcaemia — needs the fastest possible paramedic airway support.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop a high-pitched wheeze, your throat feels like it's closing, or your breathing gets much harder, tell 000 straight away to upgrade the ambulance to top priority.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A seizure (uncontrolled shaking/convulsion) or loss of consciousness
escalation_edges.rationale: Generalised seizure or collapse from severe hypocalcaemia is life-threatening in a patient who is alone — paramedic resuscitation is the critical resource.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you feel a seizure coming, get yourself to the floor away from hard objects now. If anyone is with you or the paramedics arrive, they should keep you safe and stay on the line with 000.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with active tetany — no bystander to protect the airway or summon help if the patient deteriorates or seizes
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Keep 000 on the line, unlock the front door immediately for paramedics, and have the patient sit or lie safely on the floor away from hazards. Escalation must not be softened because there is no one else present — the isolation raises, not lowers, the urgency.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance right now and tell them you have severe muscle spasms and cramping with numbness around your mouth and trouble breathing. While you wait: breathe slowly and calmly through your nose, do not eat or drink anything, sit or lie down safely away from anything you could hurt yourself on, and unlock your front door so the paramedics can get to you. Do not try to drive yourself.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 is available 24 hours a day — this is an emergency and must not wait for clinic hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary; urgent endocrinology review is needed to re-establish and titrate calcium and calcitriol replacement and to set up a reliable resupply plan so this does not recur.
